seo.title: Building Customer Support with AG2 + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with AG2 + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building customer support agents with AG2 enables sophisticated multi-agent conversations for handling tickets, CRM lookups, and RAG-based responses. However, the high-risk nature of customer interactions demands robust guardrails to prevent off-topic drifts, PII leaks, and policy violations. Vex integration provides real-time verification to ensure response accuracy, tone consistency, and safe escalations.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with AG2
architectureOverview.description:
The AG2 customer support system features a RAG agent that retrieves knowledge from a vector store, integrates with CRM for order/ticket data, and uses tool-calling for actions like ticket creation. A group chat manager coordinates between a triage agent, support specialist, and escalation agent, with speaker selection ensuring efficient conversation flow. Vex hooks intercept messages for verification before responses are sent to customers.

Key components include the RAG retriever for accurate info, CRM tools for context, and policy enforcers for compliance. The multi-agent setup handles complex queries through debate and consensus, but requires safeguards against factual errors and harmful outputs.

risks.name: Agent conversation spiraling off-topic
risks.description: Multi-agent debates can derail into irrelevant discussions, delaying ticket resolution
risks.mitigation: Vex topic drift detection with relevance scoring on message hooks

risks.name: PII leakage in responses
risks.description: CRM data exposure through unfiltered agent outputs containing customer info
risks.mitigation: Vex PII filtering scans all messages before transmission

risks.name: Inaccurate RAG responses
risks.description: Knowledge base retrieval failures leading to wrong advice or order details
risks.mitigation: Vex fact-checking against CRM ground truth and KB sources

risks.name: Tone inconsistency
risks.description: Agents producing unprofessional or aggressive responses violating brand voice
risks.mitigation: Vex sentiment analysis ensuring polite, empathetic tone

implementation.title: Set up AG2 Group Chat for Support
implementation.description: Initialize multi-agent group with RAG and CRM tools using AG2's enhanced group chat
implementation.code:
from ag2 import GroupChat, GroupChatManager
from ag2.agent import AssistantAgent

support_specialist = AssistantAgent(name="Support", llm_config={"model": "gpt-4"})
triage_agent = AssistantAgent(name="Triage", llm_config={"model": "gpt-4"})

crm_tools = [{"name": "lookup_order", "description": "Lookup customer order"}]
rag_tools = [{"name": "search_kb", "description": "Search knowledge base"}]

groupchat = GroupChat(agents=[triage_agent, support_specialist], messages=[], max_round=10)
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat, llm_config={"model": "gpt-4"})

implementation.title: RAG Integration with Vector Store
implementation.description: Configure RAG retriever for knowledge base queries in customer support context
implementation.code:
from ag2.tools import RagRetriever

rag_retriever = RagRetriever(
    vector_store="pinecone://support-kb",
    embed_model="text-embedding-ada-002",
    top_k=5,
    namespace="customer_support"
)

support_specialist.register_for_llm(name="rag_search", description="Search support KB")(rag_retriever.search)
